mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on future earnings and supports equity multiples, so a month-over-month downtrend in policy rates should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS declines for at least two consecutive observations, or if the rolling 3-year IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above a recent trend signals weakening household income and spending, so the equity market should trade lower over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the average forward return is not negative for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates expanding payroll income and retail sales, which supports near-term revenue growth and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform each single series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if negative 20-day SPX returns dominate when both series are rising.

mechanism_narrative: A strengthening INDPRO trend reflects improving industrial output and cyclical risk appetite, which should carry into higher equity prices over the next 5 trading days when production is accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO months are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal loses predictive sign in a rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5%, further policy tightening increases discount rates and recession risk, so the next 60 trading days should show negative S&P 500 performance after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold periods do not show negative 60-day forward returns on average, or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 55% across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST followed by weaker RSAFS signals a housing-led demand slowdown that tends to pressure cyclical earnings and push the S&P 500 lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls for 2 consecutive months and RSAFS also declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, a rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS signals weakening demand for goods and services, so equity returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor signal does not predict negative 60-day returns during downturn periods, or if triggered downturn episodes have positive returns in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-tightening pause, stable CPIAUCSL alongside a flat FEDFUNDS rate reduces rate-hike risk and supports equity multiple expansion, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of stable CPI and unchanged policy rates do not outperform baseline 20-day returns, or if the signal turns negative when inflation is flat and FEDFUNDS is unchanged.
